keys.description: HS256-signs the per-spawn MeetingToken AND verifies the recordings upload — the same admin secret admin-api holds (ADMIN_API_TOKEN); unset, every POST /bots would 500, so the boot refuses instead (A4)
PostgreSQL host (the meeting/session/transcript store)
PostgreSQL port
PostgreSQL database name
PostgreSQL user
PostgreSQL password (dev default; change for any non-local deploy)
explicit async SQLAlchemy URL override; when unset the URL is built from the DB_* parts (lite deliberately sets only DB_* so the asyncpg driver is chosen)

SQLAlchemy async-engine pool_size (persistent connections per pod) applied at every engine site via meeting_api.db.build_engine. Pure runtime override — reconcile with deploy/db-budget.json (Σ replicas × (pool_size + max_overflow) + reserved ≤ max_connections) on managed Postgres. #635.
SQLAlchemy async-engine max_overflow (burst connections above pool_size per pod). Pure runtime override — reconcile with deploy/db-budget.json. #635.
the transcription_segments stream + bm:meeting:{id}:status pub/sub + bot-command channel backing
the runtime kernel's runtime.v1 surface bot_spawn creates workloads on

this service's own base URL as the spawned bot must reach it (lifecycle.v1 callback + transcript socket); compose relies on the default service DNS name, helm sets it explicitly to the release-qualified Service DNS, lite overrides to localhost

keys.default: (unset — the internal lifecycle-callback header is then not attached)
keys.description: shared secret for internal service-to-service calls (x-internal-secret on the self-driven lifecycle callback)
server-side bot participant name used by POST /bots when bot_name is omitted
STT base URL the spawned bot transcribes against (the client appends /v1/audio/transcriptions) — self-hosted deploy/transcription or a hosted vexa.ai token's endpoint
bearer token for the STT service (must match that unit's API_TOKEN)

STT model id bot_spawn threads into the invocation (the OpenAI-compatible `model` form part) — required by backends that validate model ids (Groq, vLLM); ignored by the bundled deploy/transcription unit. Helm plumbing is a declared follow-up (deploy/helm/README.md) — use extraEnv there for now
deployment-level default for a spawn's transcribe_enabled when the request does not say — set false for a capture-only deployment with no STT

Comma-separated additional Jitsi deployment hostnames (a self-hosted instance whose name carries neither 'jitsi' nor a 'meet' label, e.g. calls.example.org) recognized when parsing pasted meeting links AND calendar (ICS) links; meet.jit.si, *jitsi* hosts, and (for pasted links) meet-labelled hosts are always recognized — unset ⇒ no extra hosts
deployment-level default for a spawn's recording_enabled when the request does not say

deployment-scoped authenticated-bot knob: when true, EVERY POST /bots spawn carries the invocation.v1 auth block so the bot restores the provisioned browser session (make login) and joins signed-in. Demands the BOT_USERDATA_S3_PATH + BOT_S3_* keys — incomplete config answers a typed 503 (never a silent anonymous join).

keys.default: (unset — required when BOT_AUTHENTICATED=true; the userdata prefix holding the provisioned session, e.g. userdata/bot-identity-1)
keys.description: stored-session prefix; also the per-identity serialization key (one live authenticated bot per prefix)
userdata store endpoint, as reachable FROM the bot containers (e.g. http://minio:9000)
userdata store bucket
SCOPED userdata-store access key — never the deployment's admin S3 credentials; rides the invocation env into the bot container, so its blast radius must be the userdata prefix only
scoped userdata-store secret key (see BOT_S3_ACCESS_KEY)
object-store host:port (or full http(s) URL) recordings are written to
object-store access key (recordings upload/finalize)
object-store secret key
recordings bucket
https toward the object store when true
explicit S3 endpoint override for a non-MinIO store
explicit S3 access-key override
explicit S3 secret-key override
recordings bucket for the standalone recordings composition (the unified app uses MINIO_BUCKET first)
storage-backend label stamped into the recording's meeting.data JSONB
MeetingToken lifetime — must outlive the bot's max active time (default 4h) or transcription dies mid-meeting
poll interval (s) of the transcription_segments stream consumer loop

db-writer cadence (seconds) — each tick flushes immutable live segments from the redis hash meeting:{id}:segments into the transcriptions table and drains proc:meeting:{id} notes into meeting.data (the restored 0.10 flush loop)
parent-name alias for DB_WRITER_INTERVAL_S (0.10 collector env parity) — used only when DB_WRITER_INTERVAL_S is unset

how often (seconds) the db-writer runs its self-healing O(keyspace) meeting:*:segments SCAN — startup + every this-many seconds, off the DB_WRITER_INTERVAL_S hot path. The active_meetings set is authoritative in steady state; the per-tick scan saturated redis and starved /health, restarting healthy pods (#893)
seconds a live segment must sit unmodified before the db-writer flushes it to postgres — the still-mutable tail (drafts being refined) stays in redis until it settles (0.10 parity)
TTL (seconds) re-armed on the live segment hash meeting:{id}:segments at every append — an abandoned hash cannot linger forever once flushed (0.10 parity)

bounded grace (s) for draining a finalized meeting's processed_pending entries after its view_end marker — the copilot's final post-session beat must land within this window or the drain is abandoned with a warning (ADR-0027 Train 2)
sweep interval (s) of the webhook retry-queue drain loop
tick interval (s) of the schedule.v1 job loop (drives join-retry re-spawns)
grace (s) before a stopping meeting whose workload is gone is reconciled
interval (s) of the stop-reconcile loop
grace (s) before an active meeting with a dead workload is failed by the reconciler

grace (s) before a meeting whose bot has NOT yet reached the room (requested/joining/awaiting_admission) is reconciled — derived from the lobby budget the spawn issues (waitingRoomTimeout 600s) plus headroom, so the control plane is never less patient than the deadline it handed the bot (#862)
continuous-untracked window (s): a meeting whose workload the runtime 404s for this long with no re-adoption and no bot callback is presumed lost and failed with an evidence note
uvicorn bind host (lite binds 127.0.0.1 behind its internal proxy)
uvicorn bind port
uvicorn/app log level

keys.default: (unset — calendar sync no-ops and the auto-join sweep cannot resolve the per-user cap, so it fails closed and refuses to spawn unless AUTO_JOIN_ALLOW_UNCAPPED=1)
keys.description: admin-api base URL for the internal edges the background sweeps call (/internal/users/{id}/bot-context, /internal/calendar-configs). Required on every deploy surface so calendar sync runs and auto-join spawns stay capped (#656).
auto-join sweep cadence (s) — how often scheduled meetings are checked for a due start
auto-join lead (s) — the bot is sent this long before data.scheduled_at
auto-join grace (s) — a scheduled meeting older than this is never auto-joined (no hours-late joins)
backoff (s) before an auto-join that failed loudly (cap/quota/spawn) is retried

keys.default: false — with no admin edge (ADMIN_API_URL/INTERNAL_API_SECRET) the per-user cap is unresolvable and the sweep fails closed (refuses to spawn); set 1 to deliberately allow uncapped self-host spawns
keys.description: opt-in escape hatch (#656): allow the auto-join sweep to spawn uncapped when the per-user cap cannot be resolved. Unsafe mode — chosen, never defaulted.
calendar-sync sweep cadence (s) — how often connected ICS feeds are re-fetched and upserted into planned meetings
#527: a background loop whose last heartbeat is older than this flips /health to degraded+503 (a hung consumer no longer looks alive)
#527: collector-group lag (unread transcription_segments) over this flips /health to degraded+503
#527: acked transcription_segments entries older than this are trimmed; an unread (un-acked) entry is NEVER trimmed regardless of age (the 2026-04-26 data-loss guard)
#636: per-replica Redis consumer name for the segment group; empty → derived as collector-<hostname> (pod identity) so a crashed replica's pending batch is attributable + reclaimable

#660: idle (ms) after which an abandoned per-recreate consumer (pending==0) is pruned from collector_group by the reclaim sweep (XGROUP DELCONSUMER); default 30 min, well above COLLECTOR_RECLAIM_MIN_IDLE_MS so a briefly-quiet live replica is never removed
#636: min idle (ms) before a replica reclaims another replica's un-acked segment batch via XAUTOCLAIM, so a live peer's in-flight batch is never stolen
#636: run the orphan-reclaim scan every N segment-consumer ticks
#636: /health degrades to 503 when the segment group's pending-entry (PEL) depth exceeds this — a stuck/orphaned batch is a reportable state (P18)

stt.description: spawned bots can transcribe (the STT service bot_spawn threads into the invocation.v1 payload)
stt.when_unconfigured: POST /bots with transcribe_enabled=true answers a typed 503 naming the unset key(s) — bots must opt out explicitly (transcribe_enabled=false / TRANSCRIBE_ENABLED=false) to spawn capture-only

object_storage.description: recordings persist to the object store (chunk upload + finalize → meeting.data JSONB)
object_storage.when_unconfigured: recording upload/finalize fails at request time with a storage auth error; transcripts are unaffected
